justification: All 6 ACs correctly implemented: AC1-AC2 direct status mapping, AC3 handles both FAILED and DECLINED, AC4 uses logger.warn (not logger.error) and returns 'unknown', AC5 uses toFixed(2)+parseFloat for correct decimal handling (not integer division), AC6 uses != null to cover both undefined and null. Tests cover all 6 ACs with 16 test cases; AC3 tested with both inputs; AC4 verifies logger.warn called and verifies no warn for known statuses. Logger is injected (not global). acceptance_criteria_coverage maps all 6 ACs. No external dependencies.

observations.text: AC3 explicitly tests both FAILED and DECLINED inputs
AC4 correctly uses logger.warn, includes negative test (no warn for known statuses)
AC5 uses parseFloat(toFixed(2)) pattern avoiding integer division bug, tested with values like 199->1.99
AC6 covers both undefined and null via != null guard
16 tests with edge cases beyond the minimum requirements (case-sensitivity, empty-string status, zero cents)
Logger injected as dependency, not global
acceptance_criteria_coverage present and maps all 6 ACs with test references
